description_long: This object is a cylindrical portable stereo sound system, resembling a modern Bluetooth speaker. Its main body is encased in a smooth, matte-finished shell with a prominent mesh grille covering the majority of one side, likely for optimal sound projection. Both ends are capped with slightly recessed, circular panels, one of which appears to have a port or opening, possibly for bass output or charging. On the top surface, there is a compact control panel featuring several raised buttons, likely for power, volume, and playback control. The overall design is streamlined and robust, suitable for outdoor or urban environments. The color scheme is predominantly gray, giving it a neutral and contemporary appearance.
description: A cylindrical, gray portable stereo sound system with a mesh speaker grille, recessed end caps, and a top-mounted button control panel.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the cylindrical shape, smooth shell, and the top control panel with visible buttons.
description_view_1: This angled view shows the recessed end cap with a visible port or opening and part of the mesh grille wrapping around the side.
description_view_2: The main speaker grille is fully visible, covering most of the side, with the control panel and both end caps also in view.
description_view_3: The opposite angled view mirrors view 1, showing the other end cap and the control panel, with less of the grille visible.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grille features a fine mesh pattern, likely made of woven fabric or perforated metal, with no visible decorative elements.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel consists of three raised, oval-shaped buttons arranged linearly on the top surface, with no visible labels or icons.
